title: More than 150,000 uncounted Covid-19 deaths occurred early in the pandemic, a study finds
summary: The Covid-19 pandemic's early death toll was much higher than the official U.S. count, according to a new study that spotlights dramatic disparities in the uncounted deaths.

title: STAT+: A Maryland legislator wants pharma and patient groups to disclose ties to disease awareness campaigns
summary: A bill introduced in the Maryland legislature would require drugmakers to disclose ties to disease awareness campaigns.

title: RBC: Revolution, Xenon, Arrowhead among top takeover targets
summary: As part of a larger review, analyst Leonid Timashev identified 13 companies that could see deal interest as well as more than half a dozen poised to be potential buyers.

title: J&J wins FDA nod for psoriasis pill that aims to compete with top-selling injections

title: STAT+: Clearing tumors in mice, Azalea Therapeutics advances dream of in vivo CAR-T therapy
summary: A mouse study shows CAR-T cells can be engineered in vivo with precise gene editing, a potential breakthrough that could cut costs and expand access.

title: Aspen’s fresh Parkinson’s data; Aldeyra tanks following FDA rejection
summary: Aspen Neuroscience presented positive Phase 1/2 data for its stem cell-derived therapy. Elsewhere, Aldeyra lost over half its market value following a rejection from the FDA.

title: STAT+: How ARPA-H is developing FDA-authorized AI agents, tested in clinical trials
summary: STAT's AI Prognosis takes a closer look at how the U.S. moonshot agency for health research is developing AI agents for deployment in hospitals.

summary: A Huntington's researcher treads lightly into the UniQure-FDA fray, J&J wins approval for a first-of-its kind psoriasis pill, and more in today's Readout newsletter.

title: Premature menopause is linked to 40% increase in heart disease risk
summary: JAMA Cardiology study says premature menopause, three times more common in Black women, is a risk factor for heart disease — but scientists aren't sure why.

title: As Trump administration cracks down on health care fraud, people with disabilities feel singled out
summary: As the Trump administration goes after fraud in health care, advocates say the disability community feels singled out.

title: STAT+: J&J wins approval for first-of-its-kind psoriasis pill
summary: Johnson & Johnson won U.S. approval for a daily psoriasis pill that rivals the benefits of injectable medicines and could roil a multibillion-dollar market.

title: Drug smoking can lead to severe burns, complicating harm reduction efforts
summary: Smoking instead of injecting drugs can reduce harm, but a new study shows opioid smokers are four times as likely to suffer severe burns.

summary: A buzzy venture capital firm has started raising its third fund, hoping to amass around $700 million to invest in companies that meld AI and science.

title: R1 starts up with $78M, aiming for a better kidney drug
summary: The startup believes a pill it licensed from a Chinese biotech company could be more potent, and convenient to take, than existing treatments for a common complication of chronic kidney disease.
